title: Snapshot + EvaluationGraph + node data types
analysis:
This task builds the core M2 evaluation data structures: typed graph nodes, an EvaluationGraph backed by PersistentMap, and a Snapshot with provenance tracking. The codebase already has all prerequisite types: ValueCellId/ConstraintNodeId/RealizationNodeId in reify-types/identity.rs, PersistentMap in reify-types/persistent.rs, ContentHash in reify-types/hash.rs, CompiledExpr in reify-types/expr.rs, and CompiledGeometryOp/ValueCellKind/TopologyTemplate in reify-compiler. The M1 evaluator (reify-eval/src/lib.rs) uses flat ValueMap; this task adds the graph layer alongside it without disrupting existing evaluation. New files: reify-types/src/provenance.rs (SnapshotProvenance), reify-eval/src/graph.rs (node types + EvaluationGraph), reify-eval/src/snapshot.rs (Snapshot). ValueCellKind lives in reify-compiler and will be referenced from reify-eval (which already depends on reify-compiler). SourceNodeId already exists in identity.rs with struct fields; the task spec's u64 newtype version is aspirational for later milestones, so we skip changing it. ComputeNodeId is also deferred since ComputeNode is not needed for M2.

POST-REVIEW ADDITIONS (steps 21-28, pre-4): Three blocking issues were identified in review. (1) content_hash in from_templates does not incorporate the node ID, so two nodes with identical expressions but different IDs (e.g. param A.width=80mm and param A.height=80mm) produce identical content_hashes — breaking cache invalidation. Fix: always mix ContentHash::of_str(display_id).combine(expr_hash) for all node types. (2) The realization conversion branch in from_templates is completely untested because TopologyTemplateBuilder has no .realization() builder method. Fix: add .realization() to the builder, write tests exercising the branch. (3) topology_fingerprint() merges all node-type hashes into one sorted list without type discrimination, so a value_cell with hash H aliases with a constraint of hash H. Fix: compute three per-type sub-hashes, then combine in fixed order for domain separation.

prerequisites.description: Add VersionId and SnapshotId types to crates/reify-types/src/identity.rs with derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash) and PartialOrd/Ord for VersionId. Re-export from lib.rs.

Create crates/reify-types/src/provenance.rs with SnapshotProvenance enum (Initial, Edit{changed, parent}, Elaboration{parent}, Resolution{scope, resolved, parent}). Add module declaration and re-export in lib.rs.
Create empty crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs and crates/reify-eval/src/snapshot.rs files with module declarations in lib.rs. Ensure cargo check passes.

Add Display impl for RealizationNodeId in crates/reify-types/src/identity.rs (format: '{entity}#realization[{index}]', consistent with ConstraintNodeId's Display). Add .realization(entity, index, operations: Vec<CompiledGeometryOp>) builder method to TopologyTemplateBuilder in crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs that pushes a RealizationDecl with the given id and operations. Ensure cargo check passes.

steps.description: Write unit tests for VersionId and SnapshotId in identity.rs: construction, equality, copy semantics, Debug format, VersionId ordering (PartialOrd/Ord), and use as HashMap keys.

Implement VersionId(pub u64) and SnapshotId(pub u64) in identity.rs. VersionId derives PartialOrd+Ord. Both derive Clone+Copy+Debug+PartialEq+Eq+Hash. Add Display impls. Re-export from lib.rs. Tests from step-1 should pass.

Write unit tests for SnapshotProvenance in provenance.rs: construct each variant (Initial, Edit with changed set + parent, Elaboration with parent, Resolution with scope + resolved + parent). Test Debug, Clone, PartialEq.

Implement SnapshotProvenance enum in provenance.rs with Clone+Debug+PartialEq derives. Uses HashSet<ValueCellId> for changed/resolved sets and SnapshotId for parent references. Re-export from lib.rs. Tests from step-3 should pass.
Write unit tests for ValueCellNode in graph.rs: construct with id, kind (Param/Let), cell_type, optional default_expr, content_hash. Verify all fields accessible. Test Debug and Clone.

Implement ValueCellNode struct in graph.rs with fields: id (ValueCellId), kind (ValueCellKind from reify-compiler), cell_type (Type), default_expr (Option<CompiledExpr>), content_hash (ContentHash). Derive Clone+Debug.
Write unit tests for ConstraintNodeData and RealizationNodeData in graph.rs: construct each with their respective fields (id, expr/operations, content_hash). Verify field access, Debug, Clone.

Implement ConstraintNodeData (id: ConstraintNodeId, expr: CompiledExpr, content_hash: ContentHash) and RealizationNodeData (id: RealizationNodeId, operations: Vec<CompiledGeometryOp>, content_hash: ContentHash) in graph.rs. Derive Clone+Debug.

Write unit tests for EvaluationGraph in graph.rs: create empty graph, insert nodes into value_cells/constraints/realizations PersistentMaps, get nodes back, verify len/is_empty. Test clone produces independent copy (insert into clone, original unchanged).

Implement EvaluationGraph struct with three PersistentMap fields: value_cells (ValueCellId→ValueCellNode), constraints (ConstraintNodeId→ConstraintNodeData), realizations (RealizationNodeId→RealizationNodeData). Derive Clone+Debug+Default. Tests from step-9 should pass.

Write tests for EvaluationGraph::from_templates: build a TopologyTemplate with params, lets, constraints, and realizations using TopologyTemplateBuilder. Call from_templates and verify all nodes are populated in the correct PersistentMaps with correct ids and content hashes.

Implement EvaluationGraph::from_templates(templates: &[TopologyTemplate]) -> EvaluationGraph. Iterates templates, converts ValueCellDecl→ValueCellNode, CompiledConstraint→ConstraintNodeData, RealizationDecl→RealizationNodeData, inserting each into the appropriate PersistentMap.

Write tests for EvaluationGraph::topology_fingerprint(): same graph structure produces same fingerprint, adding a node changes fingerprint, order-independent (same nodes inserted in different order produce same fingerprint).

Implement EvaluationGraph::topology_fingerprint(&self) -> ContentHash. Collects content hashes from all node maps, sorts for determinism, combines via ContentHash::combine_all. from_templates should also return the fingerprint or store it.

Write tests for Snapshot: construct with id, version, graph, values, topology_fingerprint, provenance. Test Snapshot::from_compiled_module using a CompiledModuleBuilder — verify graph nodes populated, values initialized to (Undef, Undetermined), provenance is Initial, version/snapshot ids start at 0.

Implement Snapshot struct in snapshot.rs with fields: id (SnapshotId), version (VersionId), graph (EvaluationGraph), values (PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>), topology_fingerprint (ContentHash), provenance (SnapshotProvenance). Implement Snapshot::from_compiled_module(&CompiledModule) -> Snapshot. Derive Clone+Debug.

Write tests for Snapshot O(1) clone and structural sharing: clone a snapshot, modify the clone's values map (insert a new value), verify original snapshot is unchanged (different len, original values intact). This validates PersistentMap structural sharing at the Snapshot level.

Ensure Snapshot derives Clone (leveraging PersistentMap's O(1) clone). No additional implementation needed if step-16 is correct — this step validates the structural sharing guarantee and fixes any issues found.

Write integration test in crates/reify-eval/tests/snapshot_graph.rs: use bracket_compiled_module() fixture to create Snapshot via from_compiled_module. Verify: 6 value_cells (5 params + 1 let for volume, body is realization not value cell), 3 constraints, 1 realization. Verify provenance is Initial. Clone snapshot, modify clone, verify original unchanged.
Fix any issues found in the integration test. Ensure cargo test --workspace passes with all new tests. Wire up pub exports from reify-eval/src/lib.rs for graph and snapshot modules.

Write failing tests for review Issue 1 (content_hash must incorporate node ID). In graph.rs tests, add test `content_hash_includes_node_id_for_value_cells`: build two TopologyTemplates — one with param('A','width') defaulting to literal 80mm, another with param('A','height') defaulting to the same literal 80mm. Call from_templates for each. Get the ValueCellNode for each and assert their content_hashes are NOT equal (currently they will be equal since only the expression hash is used, so this test fails). Add test `content_hash_includes_node_id_for_constraints`: two constraints with different IDs (ConstraintNodeId::new('A',0) vs ('A',1)) but identical expressions must produce different content_hashes. Add test `content_hash_no_expr_value_cell_uses_id`: a param with no default_expr should still have a non-zero content_hash derived from its ID.

Fix content_hash computation in EvaluationGraph::from_templates to always mix the node's ID into its content_hash. For ValueCellNode: `content_hash: ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", cell.id)).combine(cell.default_expr.as_ref().map(|e| e.content_hash).unwrap_or(ContentHash(0)))`. For ConstraintNodeData: `content_hash: ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", constraint.id)).combine(constraint.expr.content_hash)`. For RealizationNodeData: `content_hash: ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", realization.id)).combine(ContentHash::combine_all(realization.operations.iter().map(|op| ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{:?}", op)))))`. Tests from step-21 should now pass. Verify all existing tests still pass (existing tests check equality/inequality of fingerprints, not specific hash values, so they remain valid).

Write failing test for review Issue 2 (realization conversion branch coverage). In graph.rs tests, add test `from_templates_with_realizations`: use TopologyTemplateBuilder with the new .realization() method (from pre-4) to build a template with one param and one realization containing a single Primitive Box CompiledGeometryOp. Call from_templates and assert: graph.realizations.len() == 1, the RealizationNodeData at RealizationNodeId::new('A', 0) exists, has operations.len() == 1, and has a non-zero content_hash. Also verify the content_hash matches a manually computed value: ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", RealizationNodeId::new("A", 0))).combine(ContentHash::combine_all(ops_debug_hashes)) — this validates both the ID mixing from step-22 and the ops hashing.

Verify the realization conversion branch in from_templates works correctly with the ID-mixed content_hash from step-22. The test from step-23 should pass after pre-4 adds the builder method and step-22 fixes the hash computation. If any field mapping bugs are found in the realization loop (e.g., wrong field mapping, incorrect hash computation), fix them here. This step exercises the previously-unreachable code path end-to-end.

Write failing tests for review Issue 3 (topology_fingerprint must domain-separate node types). In graph.rs tests, add test `fingerprint_domain_separates_node_types`: manually construct graph_a with one ValueCellNode having content_hash H (e.g. ContentHash::of_str("same")) and empty constraints/realizations. Construct graph_b with empty value_cells, one ConstraintNodeData with the same content_hash H, and empty realizations. Assert graph_a.topology_fingerprint() != graph_b.topology_fingerprint() (currently equal, so test fails). Add test `fingerprint_domain_separates_all_three_types`: construct graph_c with empty value_cells/constraints and one RealizationNodeData with hash H. Assert all three fingerprints (graph_a, graph_b, graph_c) are pairwise distinct.

Fix topology_fingerprint() to domain-separate hashes by node type. Replace the single merged-and-sorted list with three independent sub-hashes: (1) collect+sort value_cell content_hashes → ContentHash::combine_all → vc_hash, (2) collect+sort constraint content_hashes → ContentHash::combine_all → cn_hash, (3) collect+sort realization content_hashes → ContentHash::combine_all → real_hash. Final fingerprint: ContentHash::combine_all([vc_hash, cn_hash, real_hash]). The fixed combine order ensures determinism and the per-bucket aggregation ensures different node-type compositions with the same hash multiset produce different fingerprints. Tests from step-25 should now pass. Verify all existing fingerprint tests still pass.

Write integration test `snapshot_from_template_with_realizations` in crates/reify-eval/tests/snapshot_graph.rs: build a CompiledModule via CompiledModuleBuilder containing one TopologyTemplate with 2 params (width, height with literal defaults), 1 constraint, and 1 realization (Primitive Box op) using the builders. Create Snapshot via from_compiled_module. Verify: graph.value_cells.len() == 2, graph.constraints.len() == 1, graph.realizations.len() == 1. Verify the realization node has correct id and 1 operation. Collect all content_hashes across all node types and assert they are all unique (no aliasing). Assert topology_fingerprint is non-zero. This provides end-to-end coverage of all three fixes working together.

Fix any issues found in the integration test from step-27. Run `cargo test --workspace` and ensure all new and existing tests pass. This is the final validation that all three blocking review issues (content_hash node-ID mixing, realization branch coverage, domain-separated fingerprint) are resolved.

design_decisions.rationale: SnapshotProvenance references only types from reify-types (ValueCellId, SnapshotId). Keeping it in reify-types avoids circular dependencies and allows other crates to reference provenance without depending on reify-eval.

design_decisions.decision: Keep ValueCellKind in reify-compiler, reference from reify-eval
design_decisions.rationale: ValueCellKind is already defined in reify-compiler and used throughout. Moving it to reify-types would be cleaner long-term but is out of scope. reify-eval already depends on reify-compiler, so the reference is safe.

design_decisions.decision: Skip SourceNodeId(u64) and ComputeNodeId(u64) newtype additions
design_decisions.rationale: SourceNodeId already exists in identity.rs with struct fields (module, declaration_index). The task description says SourceNode and ComputeNode come in later milestones. Changing SourceNodeId shape would break existing code for no M2 benefit.

design_decisions.decision: Node data types in reify-eval, not reify-types
design_decisions.rationale: ValueCellNode, ConstraintNodeData, RealizationNodeData reference CompiledExpr (reify-types) AND CompiledGeometryOp/ValueCellKind (reify-compiler). They cannot live in reify-types without introducing a reify-compiler dependency. reify-eval depends on both, making it the natural home.

design_decisions.decision: Snapshot values map uses (Value, DeterminacyState) tuple
design_decisions.rationale: Per the task spec, values in the snapshot track both the value and its determinacy state. Using a PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)> keeps it simple and leverages existing structural sharing.

design_decisions.decision: topology_fingerprint computed from sorted content hashes for determinism
design_decisions.rationale: PersistentMap iteration order is not guaranteed. Sorting hashes before combining ensures the fingerprint is deterministic regardless of insertion order, which is critical for cache invalidation.

design_decisions.decision: Initial snapshot values are (Undef, Undetermined) for all value cells
design_decisions.rationale: from_compiled_module creates the graph structure but does NOT evaluate expressions. Evaluation is a separate concern handled by the Engine. Values start as Undef/Undetermined and get populated during evaluation passes.

design_decisions.decision: Provenance uses std::collections::HashSet (not a persistent set)
design_decisions.rationale: The changed/resolved sets in SnapshotProvenance are small and created once per snapshot transition. They don't need structural sharing — regular HashSet is simpler and sufficient.

design_decisions.decision: Mix node ID into content_hash via Display string then combine (not replace)
design_decisions.rationale: Review Issue 1 fix: content_hash must be a function of both the node's identity AND its content. Using ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", id)).combine(expr_hash) ensures two nodes with identical expressions but different IDs produce different hashes, preventing stale-cache hits. The combine operation is order-dependent, so id-hash.combine(expr-hash) is deterministic. For nodes without an expression (e.g. param with no default), the expr portion uses ContentHash(0) as sentinel.

design_decisions.decision: Domain-separate topology_fingerprint by computing three per-type sub-hashes
design_decisions.rationale: Review Issue 3 fix: merging all hashes into one sorted list loses node-type information, allowing cross-type aliasing. Computing per-type sub-hashes (vc_hash, cn_hash, real_hash) independently, then combining in fixed order via ContentHash::combine_all([vc_hash, cn_hash, real_hash]) ensures that swapping a value_cell for a constraint of equal hash produces a different fingerprint. Empty buckets contribute ContentHash(0) as their sub-hash, which is the identity element of combine_all.

design_decisions.decision: Add Display impl for RealizationNodeId for consistency with other ID types
design_decisions.rationale: ValueCellId and ConstraintNodeId already have Display impls. RealizationNodeId needs one so the same content_hash pattern (ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", id))) works uniformly across all node types. Format: '{entity}#realization[{index}]' mirrors ConstraintNodeId's '{entity}#constraint[{index}]'.

reuse.how: Used as the backing store for all EvaluationGraph node maps and Snapshot values map. Provides O(1) clone via structural sharing.

Used for topology_fingerprint computation and content_hash fields on all node data types. combine() mixes node ID hash with expression/content hash. combine_all() aggregates per-bucket sub-hashes and final fingerprint.

Used as keys in EvaluationGraph PersistentMaps and as identifiers in node data types. Their Display impls provide deterministic string representations used in content_hash computation via ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", id)).
CompiledExpr stored in node data types. Value+DeterminacyState in Snapshot values map. Type in ValueCellNode.

reuse.what: TopologyTemplate, ValueCellDecl, CompiledConstraint, RealizationDecl, ValueCellKind, CompiledGeometryOp
reuse.how: Input to EvaluationGraph::from_templates. ValueCellDecl fields mapped to ValueCellNode fields. CompiledConstraint to ConstraintNodeData. RealizationDecl to RealizationNodeData.
Used in tests to construct TopologyTemplate and CompiledModule fixtures. Extended with .realization() method in pre-4 to enable realization branch testing.
Used in integration test (step-19) to verify Snapshot creation from a realistic compiled module.

reuse.what: Expression builder helpers (literal, value_ref, gt, lt)
reuse.how: Used to construct CompiledExpr values for test node data types and template builders.
